artPrompt: The dramatic moment of hastening doom: two clawed hands wrench the great blank face of a looming clock tower forward, gold gears spilling sparks as the hands lurch, ashen congregants below raising arms in feverish glee beneath a blood-dark sky. Smoke and motion blur the strain. Tall wide sweep, bone white and cold gold washes bleeding into deep black. Rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Winding the Clock Forward
text: Put two doom counters on the Doomsday Clock. Then you draw a card for each two doom counters on it, rounded down. Hastening the end has never been so rewarding.
